title: Where Trees Haven't Learned to Read

features.name: young-saplings
features.desc-on-touch: small trees in orderly rows, not yet comparing themselves to their past rings. their bark is smooth, unreadable, still learning to remember

features.name: proto-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: marks on bark that might become meaning, or might just be scars. they're too young to have decided yet

features.name: memory-gap
features.desc-on-touch: an open space between the old and young stands, where the difference is visible. the young trees don't yet know they're supposed to compare themselves to what came before

features.name: first-reading
features.desc-on-touch: a single tree, just beginning. you can see it learning. the process is slow and doesn't hurt, yet

inscription: the young trees are still innocent of comparison. they don't yet know that rain from thirty years ago is falling through them right now

file.name: Saplings_PreCorruption_Log.txt
file.note: observations of trees that haven't started reading themselves yet. once written, this file began aging at accelerated speed—just from being observed

exits.label: back to the weathered stand where reading has been happening for decades
toward open country where no archive has reached yet
downstream toward rivers that still only flow, not compare
